examples: Sandstones generally make good aquifers, while finer-grained mudstones are typically impermeable.
Beneath this active layer is a second layer called "permafrost", which is frozen all year around, and is impermeable to water.
The canoe is made from an impermeable wood.
It insists that the Chinese walls separating its traders and bankers are always impermeable.
The landfill sits on a natural foundation of London clay which is more or less impermeable.
